Q14 NAT Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +1 / -0
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:
Statement-I: In an AVL tree, if the closest leaf to the root is at level k, then all nodes at level 1 to k – 2 will have 2 children.
Statement-II: In an AVL tree, if the closest leaf to the root is at level k, then the node at level k – 1 (its parent) may have 1 child.
Find the number of correct statements.

NAT
Correct answer: 2
Solution

2


• If a node at level k – 2 does not have the 2nd child then height imbalance will be created. So Statement-I should be true.
• Node at level k – 1 is allowed to have a single child that is the leaf node at level k. Statement-II is also true. Node at level k – 1 may have either 1 child or both the children.

Q15 MCQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

During an Active FTP session, a user can successfully log in to the FTP server and list directory contents, but file uploads and downloads fail. Which of the following is most likely cause?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(a)
In Active FTP, the server initiates the data connection back to the client. This means:
Control connection: Client → Server port 21
Data connection: Server port 20 → Client (random port specified via PORT command)
If the client’s firewall blocks incoming connections, the server’s attempt to create the data channel
fails.

Q19 MCQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Consider the following relational schemes: R(A, B, C, D, E, F) and S(A, B, C) within the following functional dependencies: {AB → C, C → ABDE, ADE → F}. Assume {A, B} is the key for both schemes. Which of the following statements is true?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
R is in 2NF and S is in 3NF and R covers S.
Because for the relation R FD’s are {AB → C, C → ABDE, ADE → F} out of these 3 FD’s 2nd one
is giving (non-key → non-key) so it is not in 3NF, but there are no partial dependencies, you
might be confused in 3rd FD (but it not partial FD).
Regarding relation S, it is in BCNF means it is also in 3NF right.
So option (c) is more appropriate.

Q23 MCQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:
S1 : Control hazard penalties eliminated by dynamic branch prediction.
S2 : Stall is a extra cycle created in the pipeline with NOP.
Which of the above statements is correct?

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
• Dynamic branch prediction reduces control hazard penalties but cannot eliminate them completely, because mispredictions still occur. Hence, penalties are reduced, not eliminated. So, S1 is incorrect.
• A stall (or pipeline bubble) means the pipeline inserts a NOP instruction (no operation), which effectively adds an extra cycle delay. So, S2 is correct.

Q37 MCQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Which of the following is true for a Turing Machine?

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
Halting, state-entry, and finiteness problem are standard undecidable problems for a Turing Machine.
Every TM M have infinite TM which are equivalent to M. So, option (d) is decidable using Rice’s
theorem. As, property in (d) is always false, so it is trivial property.

Q46 MCQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Alisha, Babita and Catty go to a Chinese restaurant at a busy time of the day. The waiter apologetically explains that the restaurant can provide only two pairs of chopsticks (for a total of four chopsticks) to be shared among the three people. Alisha proposes that all four chopsticks be
placed in an empty glass at the center of the table and that each diner should obey the following protocol:

 
 
Consider the following statements:
1. This dining plan may lead to deadlock.
2. This dining plan may lead to starvation of someone.
Which of the above is correct?
Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
Deadlock cannot occur because there are enough chopsticks to guarantee that at least one of the
3 diners will be able to get the 2 chopsticks that he/she needs. Once that diner finishes, he/she
will release their chopsticks for someone else to use, so eventually everyone finishes. Starvation
is possible if two people keep on getting the chopsticks again and again.

Q50 MSQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Suppose we want to synchronize two concurrent processes P and Q using binary semaphores A and B. The code for the processes ‘P’ and ‘Q’ is as shown below

 
 
Which one of the following statement is correct with respect to the above scenario?
Correct answer: C, A, B
Solution

(c)
At A = 0, B = 1, (101)n will be printed.
Hence option (a) and (b) are incorrect.
Considering, A = 1, B = 0, initially process P will execute and process Q will be blocked hence ‘01’
will be printed. Then, after V(B), process ‘Q’ will execute print ‘1’ and will wake process P.
Hence, 011 will be printed in loop.

Q56 MSQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Which of the following statements are true?

Correct answer: B, C, D
Solution

(b, c, d)
• If disk block allocated for B+ tree index and same size disk block is allocated for B tree index.
Then B tree index access cost is greater than B+ tree index for given distinct keys. So, option (a) is incorrect.
• If every candidate key will be simple, then there will be no partial dependency, hence relation will be in 2NF. So, option (c) is correct.
• If every attribute is prime, then there will be no transitive dependency, hence the relation will always be in 3NF. So, option (d) is correct.

Q62 MCQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider a relational database schema with two tables:
1. DEPARTMENTS
• DeptID (Primary Key, NOT NULL)
• DeptName
2. EMPLOYEES
• EmpID (Primary Key, NOT NULL)
• EmpName
• Mgr ID (Foreign Key referencing EmpID in EMPLOYEES)
• Dept ID (Foreign Key referencing DeptID in DEPARTMENTS)
Now, assume the following two specific constraints are applied to the foreign key on
EMPLOYEES.DeptID:
1. EMPLOYEES.DeptID is constrained as NOT NULL.
2. The foreign key definition includes the Referential Action: ON DELETE SET NULL.
A new department is created with DeptID = 100. An employee is hired and assigned to this
department: EmpID = 500, DeptID = 100.
Which of the following database operations will result in a Referential Integrity Error?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
1. The DELETE operation targets the department that is currently referenced by EmpID = 500.
2. The Foreign Key’s Referential Action is triggered: ON DELETE SET NULL.
3. The database attempts to automatically change the EMPLOYEES. DeptID value from 100 to
NULL.
4. This attempt immediately violates the NOT NULL constraint explicitly placed on the
EMPLOYEES.DeptID column.
5. Because the database fails to execute the referential action, the original DELETE statement is
rolled back and an Referential Integrity Error is raised.
This operation tests the most advanced interaction: that a NOT NULL constraint on the referencing
column overrides an ON DELETE SET NULL action.

A non-pipelined processor has average CPI of 3 cycles and clock rate of 2 GHz. After upgrade, processor converted to 5 stage pipeline to latencies 0.3 ns, 0.4 ns, 0.45 ns, 0.75 ns, 0.55 ns. Consider a program has 30% branch instruction and it lead to 2 stage penalty. All other instruction have no penalties. Assume program has large number of instructions. Approximate speedup achieved by new processor over old processor on execution of this program is

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)

The following nodes are inserted into an AVL tree (initially empty):
51, 22, 61, 11, 9, 16, 33, 47, 12, 49
The height of the resulting tree is equal to ________.

NAT
Correct answer: 3
Solution

3
